title: Articulated Memory-Shell Rib
description: A monumental curved rib from the world's memory shell, combining the anatomy of a whale skeleton, the precision of an observatory truss, and the ceremonial finish of a reliquary. Each rib is modular enough to stand alone, interlock into a shell, or carry smaller remembered objects in suspended satellite cages.
narrativeFunction: The rib gives the world a protective but incomplete enclosure. It implies that memories are held inside body-like architecture without becoming a literal creature. Repeated ribs establish scale, frame camera views, carry lights and satellites, and create strong near-to-far parallax.

orientation.originMeaning: The origin sits at the center of the lower foundation shoe so the rib can be planted, rotated, mirrored, or snapped around a world-shell baseline.

silhouette.primary: A tall inward-curving arc that begins thick and load-bearing at the base, narrows through the middle, and forks into two delicate prongs near the crown.
silhouette.secondary: Seven visible vertebral plates interrupt the continuous arc, each offset enough to expose hinge gaps and a dark internal tendon running through the center.
silhouette.tertiary: Gold repair seams, socket caps, hanging satellite loops, engraved memory dates, fine tension cables, and bearing indicators accumulate around joints rather than uninterrupted surfaces.
silhouette.negativeSpace: The inside of the curve is one large uninterrupted opening. Hinge gaps and the forked crown create smaller repeated slivers of darkness visible at medium distance.
silhouette.recognitionTest: In silhouette the asset reads as one anatomical architectural rib with a planted foot, segmented spine, and forked crown, not as a generic arch, branch, tusk, or complete ring.

materials.surfaceDescription: Dense ivory-gray stone with faint translucent edges, longitudinal growth lines, chipped corners, and fractures repaired by narrow metallic seams.
Dark bronze-black hardware with polished bearing surfaces, oxidized recesses, visible fasteners, and warm indicators at articulation pins.
A semi-translucent braided energy tendon with visible internal fiber direction and brighter nodes where it crosses each mechanical joint.

components.role: Heavy planted base transferring shell load into terraces or invisible world structure.
components.detail: Chamfer the footprint, model four recessed anchor wells, expose one service plate, and repair one side with a visible but restrained gold seam.

components.role: Seven tapered curved shell segments forming the main anatomical arc.
components.detail: Each segment tapers upward, flattens slightly toward the shell interior, and contains one shallow longitudinal channel that aligns imperfectly across hinges.

components.role: Exposed articulation pins and plates positioned between shell segments.
components.detail: Each joint has one emissive central bearing, two unequal retaining collars, and a visible stop limiting excessive articulation.

components.role: Continuous internal tension line visible through every segment gap.
components.detail: The tendon remains visually continuous, bows toward the open interior between joints, and thickens slightly at articulation nodes.

components.role: Attachment loops for photographs, lanterns, or carried-memory cages.
components.detail: Loops alternate sides and hang from reinforced tabs. Each loop includes a clasp break and a small engraved ownership mark.

assemblyOrder: Build foundation shoe and lower bearing
Thread luminous tendon through every joint
Attach satellite loops and repair seams
deformationRules: The complete rib bends inward approximately thirty-four degrees from base to crown while preserving a stable planted foot.
Each segment rotates two to six degrees relative to the previous one to expose hinge gaps without breaking the overall arc.
The crown bifurcates into two prongs whose tips diverge by twenty-two centimeters and lean into different depth directions.
variationRules: Foundation repair seams may move between left and right faces but remain visually subordinate to the silhouette.
Individual segment lengths vary by eight percent while total assembled height remains within three percent of target.
Satellite loop count ranges from three to five and avoids perfectly regular vertical spacing.

topology.target: Game-ready modular hard-surface and curve-derived topology with separate segment, hinge, tendon, and foundation objects for rigging and replacement.
topology.bevel: Use broad stone edge softening, narrower bronze bevels, and distinct polished bearing bevels; do not round the complete rib into an organic tube.
topology.subdivision: Use limited subdivision on bone-stone profiles and tendon only. Hinge plates, footing, and repair bands remain controlled hard-surface forms.
topology.normals: Weighted normals preserve calm carved planes while hinge gaps, repair seams, and structural edges carry readable highlights.
topology.uv: Use longitudinal UV flow on shell segments, trim-sheet hardware UVs, and a dedicated emissive mask for tendon fibers and bearing indicators.

animation.motionCharacter: A nearly imperceptible breathing articulation. Upper joints flex first, lower joints answer later, and the luminous tendon brightens as the shell tightens. Motion communicates structural life rather than rubber deformation.
constraints: Joint rotation stays below three degrees during idle motion.
Crown prongs never touch adjacent shell ribs.

collision.description: Use the foundation box plus three tapered capsules following the primary arc. Satellite loops, tendon, cables, and crown tips remain non-colliding.

sockets.purpose: Placement socket for terraces, shell rings, or world foundations.
Optional connection to a shell spine or overhead tension cable.
Attachment point for one hero carried-memory cage.

orthographicGuidance.front: Show the full inward arc, seven segment breaks, wide planted shoe, and forked crown. The tendon appears as a thin inner line crossing every joint.
orthographicGuidance.side: Reveal the depth bow, alternating hinge plates, crown-prong separation, and satellite loops offset from the shell surface.
orthographicGuidance.top: Emphasize the flattened inner face, thicker outer spine, and slight corkscrew progression of segment rotations.
orthographicGuidance.threeQuarter: Frame from the open interior looking toward the rib so hinge gaps, gold repairs, and carried-memory sockets are readable against negative space.
orthographicGuidance.scaleCue: Place a human at the foundation shoe; the first hinge sits above head height and the crown reaches roughly four human heights.

validation: The assembled rib has exactly seven primary shell segments and six visible hinge intervals.
The planted base is wider in depth than the mid-rib and reads as a structural footing.
The internal tendon remains visible through at least five hinge gaps from the hero camera.
The crown visibly forks and does not terminate as one blunt point.
No satellite loop intersects shell geometry during bind pose or idle animation.
LOD2 preserves the overall inward arc, foundation shoe, forked crown, and tendon line.
Mirrored instances remain plausible without becoming perfectly identical.
